sanskrit_devanagari:
प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः ।
अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताहमिति मन्यते ॥ २७ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāni
guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ |
ahaṅkāra-vimūḍhātmā
kartāham iti manyate || 27 ||

sanskrit_sources.agreement: exact (raw HTML scrape; akṣara sequence as published)
exact (text body identical with vedabase; punctuation rendering differs)
exact (text body identical; punctuation rendering differs)
exact (academic critical edition; cross-reference Bhg_03.027 = MBh_06,025.027). Body text identical; word-segmentation differs by edition.

translations.verbatim_quote: The spirit soul bewildered by the influence of false ego thinks himself the doer of activities that are in actuality carried out by the three modes of material nature.
(See holy-bhagavad-gita.org for full Mukundananda rendering; agreement with Prabhupada noted in disagreements section where it diverges materially.)

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: Two persons, one in Kṛṣṇa consciousness and the other in material consciousness, working on the same level, may appear to be working on the same platform, but there is a wide gulf of difference in their respective positions.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.27 Karmani kriyamanani, while actions, secular and scriptural, are being done; sarvasah, in ever way; gunaih, by the gunas, (i.e.) by the modifications in the form of body and organs; (born) prakrteh, of Nature-Nature, (otherwise known as) Pradhana [Pradhana, Maya, the Power of
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara: prakṛteḥ guṇaiḥ kriyamāṇāni karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ — actions are in every respect done by the gunas of prakṛti. The Self is asaṅga, untouched by action. The ahaṅkāra-vimūḍha-ātmā — the one whose self-conception is deluded by the ego that mistakes the body-mind complex for the Self — thinks 'kartāham — I am the doer.' This is misknowing in the strict sense; the realised one sees the gunas as the agents and the Self as the witness.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.27 It is the Gunas of Prakrti like Sattva, Rajas etc., that perform all the activities appropriate to them. But the man, whose nature is deluded by his Ahankara, thinks, ‘I am the doer of all these actions.’ Ahankara is the mistaken conception of ‘I’ applied to the workings of P
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja: the gunas of prakṛti, in their characteristic combinations, perform all action. The jīva (the embodied self) is the locus of experience but not the agent of action in the metaphysical sense. The deluded jīva — ahaṅkāra-vimūḍha — attributes the action of the gunas to itself and so claims kartāham. The realised jīva sees through this and acts without claiming the agency.

positions.rendering: the spirit soul bewildered by the influence of false ego thinks himself the doer of activities that are in actuality carried out by the three modes of material nature
the soul deluded by the ego thinks itself to be the doer of activities that are in fact carried out by the three modes of nature

the Self in truth is unchanging and not the doer; the gunas of prakṛti are what perform action; the ahaṅkāra-vimūḍha mistakes the body-mind complex for the Self and thereby attributes doership where it does not belong

the jīva, when deluded by ahaṅkāra, attributes doership to itself rather than to the gunas of prakṛti; in truth the jīva is acted-through by the gunas, and the realised one sees this and acts without claiming agency

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: The Advaita and Vishishtadvaita readings agree that the kartāham claim is false but disagree on what the not-doer is. For Shankara, the true Self (Brahman, ātman) is the not-doer; the gunas operate in the field of māyā. For Ramanuja, the jīva is real but is acted-through by the gunas; the doership-claim is wrong but the jīva itself is not unreal. Translators flatten both to 'soul / spirit soul' which obscures the distinction.

literal_meaning: Actions are in every way done by the gunas of prakṛti; but the one whose self is deluded by ahaṅkāra (ego) thinks 'I am the doer.'
traditional_meaning_consensus: One of the chapter's metaphysically densest verses. Krishna names the metaphysics: the gunas (sattva-rajas-tamas, the three modes of prakṛti) are the actual agents of action; the embodied actor's claim 'I am the doer' is ahaṅkāra-vimūḍha — a delusion produced by ego. The verse must be handled carefully — its claim is doctrinal, not motivational.
